title: After-Action Report — Pre-Modern Ballpark Revival + Park-Shape Gameplay Hook
scope: User came back to the spray-chart work with a design fiat: "Imagine a revival of the original ballparks rather than there ever being an era of cookie-cutter parks. I love urban stadiums but also cavernous fields like old Cleveland Stadium, Forbes Field, Polo Grounds and others like that."

rationale.item: Design fiat: revival of original ballparks, no cookie-cutter era; Tal's Hill-style quirks wanted.

Mid-batch second swing: "the idea of playing baseball in Ovals appeals to me a lot because it'd be an interesting way to reimagine how it'd impact hitting" — added a 7th shape (oval / cricket-ground revival).

delegation_events.decision: Gameplay-hook scope: option B (no per-shape balancing) + all modifiers, no follow-ons
delegation_events.quote: User picked **option B (no per-shape balancing) + all modifiers, no follow-ons**.
Added a 7th shape (oval / cricket-ground revival) to the original 6.

delegation_events.decision: Hook call site after talent-flex but before Stay decision
delegation_events.quote: The "where to call the hook" decision (after talent-flex but before Stay) was the only non-obvious design call.

actions.description: 7 weighted park shape archetypes driving dimension distributions, shape-tied wall heights

20-item architectural quirk catalog, 0-3 per park weighted (0.38, 0.40, 0.17, 0.05), some shape-gated; persisted as JSON in new teams.park_quirks column; gold tag chips on team page and spray-chart subtitle

Spray chart upgrade: SVG draws the home park's real fence geometry from park_dimensions with bezier control points, wall-height stroke scaling, dimension labels; falls back to symmetric arc on legacy rows

Park-shape gameplay hook: new o27/engine/park_effects.py with apply_park_effects and five rewrite rules (HR upgrade, HR downgrade, picket-fence robbery, cavernous gappers, oval tweener triples); state.park_dimensions field; sim.py stamps it; prob.py calls hook after resolve_contact + talent-flex, before Stay decision
Distance heuristic refactor: SVG and gameplay share _proxy_distance(ev, la); divisor tuned 36 → 25 against Statcast medians; _bip_distance_ft delegates to it
Additive schema: teams.park_shape, teams.park_quirks (idempotent ALTER TABLEs + SCHEMA, NULL-tolerant)

risk_posture: Identity no-op when `park_dims` is None / empty.
Both idempotent ALTER TABLEs + SCHEMA. NULL-tolerant.
Per-shape balancing intentionally absent (option B per user direction).
The 5-run R/G spread by shape is the feature, not a calibration miss.
the rules are ordered to bias the right direction (upgrade → downgrade → robbery → gapper)

validation_claims.claim: 120-game reseed sample by home park shape: balanced 2.28 HR/G ... cavernous 0.67 HR/G (3.4× HR spread); bathtub 8.20 2B/G league-leading; oval 7.42 2B/G + 1.31 3B/G; league R/G holds in the 22-26 README band at every shape (20.5 to 25.5)
validation_claims.check_method: Reseed and sim 120 games, group HR/2B/3B/R by home park shape

validation_claims.claim: Smoke test (python o27v2/smoke_test.py): 10/10 PASS after the hook

non_actions.reason: intentionally absent (option B per user direction). ... The whole point of the design fiat is that parks differ; a rebalanced surface would have ironed out the variety.
The data is there ... but no UI exists for it yet.

non_actions.item: Quirks wired into the engine (Tal's Hill slope, Wire Basket doubles, Bullpens-in-Play errors)
non_actions.reason: Quirks are visual-only. ... None of those wired into the engine.

non_actions.item: Physical projectile model (air resistance, altitude, wind)
non_actions.reason: Distance heuristic is shared but still non-physical.

non_actions.item: pitching_log / batter splits filtered by park shape
non_actions.reason: The pa_log has `game_id`, joinable to `teams.park_shape`, but no UI presents the split.

non_actions.item: Switch-hit batter side-of-plate decision consulting park dimensions
non_actions.reason: The engine treats `bats='S'` as handedness-flexible but doesn't currently consult park dimensions in the side-of-plate decision.

residual_risks: League standings will tilt toward teams whose home parks favor their roster, which is a real strategic axis the GM layer doesn't currently model.
The hook overrides talent-flex adjustments — compounds two systems on the same BIP.
The hook fires once per BIP — the first rule that fires wins.
open_hooks: Park-shape leaderboards ("Best Hitter at Cavernous Parks", "Pitcher's Parks W-L Splits")
Real projectile model making weather effects meaningful
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Pre-Modern Ballpark Revival + Park-Shape Gameplay Hook
3. Spray chart upgrade — actual fence geometry
4. Park-shape gameplay hook — the headline feature
unmapped_content: Branch claude/review-and-improve-NbhPy. Shape table (balanced 40%, short_porch_rf 10%, short_porch_lf 7%, cavernous 13%, bathtub 8% CF 478', triangle 10%, oval 12% lines ~380') with real-world inspirations; wall-height rules (bathtub/short-porch 45% chance 28-50 ft; ovals 60% chance 4-8 ft cricket-tin); sample seed parks (Sunset Loukas Stadium bathtub LF278/CF462/RF292; The Oval LF377/CF421/RF378 6' picket fence; Vancouver Bowl 47' wall). Quirk lists (universal and shape-gated). Five rewrite-rule details (HR upgrade LA 18-42° clearing fence at spray angle; HR downgrade to wall doubles, ≥25 ft walls need extra carry; ~18% picket robbery on ≤7 ft walls; cavernous gappers promote singles in ≥415 ft alleys, 440+/290+ produce triples; 22% oval tweener triples fence ≥380 ft ball ≥320 ft off-center). Files-changed diffstat block. Reused vs new lists. Process notes: scope iterated twice mid-session, same migration scaffold kept; distance-divisor mistake (36 vs ~25) produced 1 HR in 120 games on first calibration, fixed in one edit; refactoring _bip_distance_ft to call _proxy_distance prevents silent SVG/gameplay drift.
parser_notes: Date from 'Date completed:' line. Slug shortened to 8 words from long title.
